description: ExposoGraph 3.0 causal motif library (Phase 4: promoted to active)

motifs.name: Exposure → Metabolite → Target Damage
motifs.description: Carcinogen undergoes metabolic activation to a reactive metabolite that damages a molecular target (e.g. DNA adduct). Covers both the bioactivation step (activates) and the adduct-forming step (damages).

motifs.name: Detoxification / Competing Elimination
motifs.description: Enzyme or pathway detoxifies a reactive metabolite or substrate, competing with activation and reducing effective carcinogenic burden. Includes GST-mediated conjugation, hydrolysis, glucuronidation, methylation, and competitive inhibition.

motifs.name: Pathway → Phenotype / Outcome
motifs.description: A biological pathway step or transport event precedes or mediates a downstream tissue phenotype or outcome. Covers transport processes that position metabolites for further reaction, and pathway nodes that lead to phenotypic outcomes.

motifs.name: Modifier → Susceptibility
motifs.description: A host-side modifier (germline variant, allele, polymorphism, or comorbidity state) modifies enzyme activity or pathway capacity, altering individual susceptibility to carcinogenic exposure. Formalizes genotype-phenotype relationships in the registry.

motifs.name: Tissue Context → Outcome
motifs.description: Tissue-specific expression context (e.g. GTEx-derived weighting) modifies enzyme activity and determines the tissue-level carcinogenic outcome. Formalizes tissue-context graph views from ExposoGraph 2.0.

motifs.name: Repair → Damage Mitigation
motifs.description: A DNA repair enzyme or pathway mediates removal or reversal of carcinogen-induced DNA damage, reducing adduct burden and downstream mutagenic risk. Covers BER, NER, direct-reversal, and FA-pathway repair.

motifs.name: Induction → Increased Activation
motifs.description: A receptor or transcription factor (e.g. AHR) is induced by an exposure and in turn up-regulates metabolic activation enzymes (e.g. CYP1A1, CYP1B1), amplifying downstream bioactivation flux. Covers INDUCES-type regulatory edges.
